headline: Stocks to Watch Thursday: Apple, Exxon, ASML
summary: ↘️ Apple (AAPL), Nvidia (NVDA), Tesla (TSLA): The three tech stocks were the most heavily traded S&P 500 components before the U.S. bell. All fell, weighing on index futures. ↘️ UniCredit (IT:UCG), Deutsche Bank (XE:DB), Santander (ES:SAN): European bank stocks fell as government bond yields in the region rose, weighing on the price of securities on the lenders' books.

headline: Tesla Stock Is Falling. It Isn’t the Delivery Numbers.
summary: Tesla  stock fell in premarket trading Thursday, ahead of its release of first-quarter delivery figures, as investors grew nervous about renewed conflict in the Middle East.  Benchmark international crude oil prices were up 8% in early trading on Thursday, above $109 per barrel.  Coming into Thursday trading, the S&P 500 was up 3.2% for the week, leaving it down 4.4% since the fighting in Iran began.

content.title: Tesla Stock Is Falling. It Isn’t the Delivery Numbers.
content.summary: Tesla  stock fell in premarket trading Thursday, ahead of its release of first-quarter delivery figures, as investors grew nervous about renewed conflict in the Middle East.  Benchmark international crude oil prices were up 8% in early trading on Thursday, above $109 per barrel.  Coming into Thursday trading, the S&P 500 was up 3.2% for the week, leaving it down 4.4% since the fighting in Iran began.

headline: Trump Budget to Frame Midterms With Defense Boost, Domestic Cuts
summary: (Bloomberg) -- President Donald Trump is preparing to release a fiscal year 2027 budget plan on Friday that will frame his party’s midterm election message around a massive defense buildup, partially paid for by cuts to domestic agencies and health-care entitlements. A governing vision that directs tax dollars to the Pentagon amid an unpopular Iran war represents a political risk for the White House, especially against a backdrop of spiking gasoline prices. Trump could also face resistance from

headline: A $5.8 Billion Reason To Rethink Rivian Stock
summary: While other EV companies struggle to sell more vehicles in a slowing demand environment, Rivian (RIVN) may have quietly found a smarter game. Volkswagen (VWAGY) - one of the world's largest automakers - is building toward deploying Rivian's software stack across 30 million vehicles globally, spanning VW, Audi, and Scout brands. After years of painful internal delays at its own software unit, VW effectively concluded that building the stack itself wasn't going too well. Rivian's architecture was

headline: The 30% Margin Cash Cow Funding Tesla’s Trillion-Dollar AI Bet
summary: Tesla's (TSLA) long-term valuation now rests on two AI bets: the Cybercab robotaxi network and the Optimus humanoid robot. The problem? Both are currently cost centers, and that means the company needs cash. So where does it find it? The answer might surprise you.

content.title: The 30% Margin Cash Cow Funding Tesla’s Trillion-Dollar AI Bet
content.summary: Tesla's (TSLA) long-term valuation now rests on two AI bets: the Cybercab robotaxi network and the Optimus humanoid robot. The problem? Both are currently cost centers, and that means the company needs cash. So where does it find it? The answer might surprise you.

headline: This Week In Energy Transition - Fengate Expands Solar Portfolio with Major Joint Venture
summary: Fengate Asset Management has expanded its renewable energy portfolio by acquiring a 50% interest in a 227 MWac portfolio of 12 operational solar projects across the western United States. This acquisition forms part of a joint venture with Clearway Energy Inc., enhancing Fengate's strategic focus on energy transition initiatives. Managed under the Fengate Infrastructure Yield Fund, this investment aligns with the company's objective to engage in long-term contracted renewable projects,...
